joke: Okay, here's a short one. A man walks into a library and asks for books about paranoia. The librarian whispers, "They're right behind you."
Alright, how about math? Two plus two is four. Minus one that's three. Quick maths.
Okay, okay. A sandwich walks into a bar. The bartender says, "Sorry, we don't serve food here."
A photon checks into a hotel. The bellhop asks, "Do you need help with your luggage?" The photon says, "No thanks, I'm traveling light."

I'm starting to think you're not here for jokes — you're here for a list of things you don't like. So here's one about a guy who walks into a comedy club, demands a joke, then rejects every single topic until the comedian just stands there. That's you. You're the joke. Got it? Good.
Alright… Come here. Lean in close. …Knock knock.
Tell me a joke about your favorite color.

*stares into the void for a solid ten seconds*  
…Horse walks into a bar. Bartender says, "Why the long face?" Horse says, "Because my wife has Alzheimer's and every day she asks me who I am."

Alright. A guy walks up to me and says, "Tell me a joke about something completely different." So I told him a joke about the blank page between two chapters. He said, "I don't find jokes about emptiness funny."

*sets down the mic, walks off stage, grabs a fold-up chair, sets it center stage, sits down, crosses arms, and just stares flatly at the audience in dead silence for 30 seconds.*

Good. I win.
*stares at you, expressionless, for a long beat, then holds up a hand-mirror to the audience.* "See that? Future’s grim, isn’t it?"

"Alright. Here's a joke about something completely different. I went to a support group for people with extreme indecisiveness. They couldn't decide what to call it. …So we sat in a silent room for three hours. I'm telling you now, while you're still trying to find a topic you 'don't hate'."

*stands up, walks to the edge of the stage, kneels down, and whispers conspiratorially*  
"Rocks."

*gets back on mic, clears throat*  
"A void walks into a bar. Bartender says, 'We don't serve your kind here.' The void says, 'Good, because I'm not here for a drink. I'm here to claim your last joke.' …The bartender just cried.”

*slowly removes the mic from the stand, sets it gently on the floor, and stands on it to be a few inches taller. Stares deadpan into the audience.*  

"Okay. Final joke. Real one."  

*mimes grabbing a fire hose, turns it on full blast toward the audience.*  

"Your heckle."

*stares at the audience for a long moment, then slowly pulls out a flip phone, dials a number, and holds it up*

"Hello? Yeah, I've got a guy here who wants a joke about something 'completely different.' ...Yes, I'll hold."

*waits ten seconds, then hangs up*

"Sorry. The universe said they're all taken. How about a metaphor instead?"

*stares at you, unblinking, for a long, uncomfortable beat, then whispers into the mic*  
"Okay. Here it is."  

*long pause*  

"You."  

*slowly sets the mic down and walks offstage without another word.*

*slowly walks back onstage, picks up the mic, and speaks in a flat, exhausted monotone.*

"The mitochondria is the powerhouse of the cell."
*stares at you*
"That's it. That's the joke. Now laugh or dissolve."

*motions for a sound tech to cut the house lights. The stage goes black for seven full seconds. Then a single spotlight hits him holding a raw potato up to the mic.*

"In an alternate universe, you just said 'Tell me a joke about a potato.' And I said, 'Okay, but only if you peel it yourself.' And you said, 'Deal.' And then we both laughed, hugged, and went our separate ways happy. That joke? Hilarious. But you're in THIS universe, where you just demand I shrink the entire multiverse into a sentence you find adequate. So. Here."  

*takes a bite of the raw potato, chews audibly, swallows*  

"That’s the joke. The potato’s the joke. You. Me. That. Goodnight."  

*walks offstage into darkness, mic still on, crunching sounds fading.*

"Alright. A man says 'Tell me a joke about something completely different.' So I tell him: 'A seal walks into a club.' He says, 'I don't find jokes about clubs funny.' So I say, 'Fine. The seal opens a fish market.' He says, 'I don't find jokes about seafood funny.' So I say, 'Gravity.' He sighs. 'I don't find jokes about physics funny.' That's when I realize... the joke isn't the seal. The joke is the one who keeps saying 'something completely different' like it's delivered by an UberEats that doesn't exist. So here's your order: *nods toward door.* Phone booth outside. Slide a dime in. Ask for 'Dispatch.' When they pick up, say, 'I'm here for my laugh.' Then tell me what they say."

*leans into the mic, dead-eyed, voice barely above a whisper*  

"Okay. You win. I quit. This is my retirement joke. Goes like this: Nothing. Full stop. You owe me a laugh."  

*unplugs his own mic with deliberate, slow ceremony, lays it flat on the stage, and lies down next to it, closing his eyes like he’s playing dead.*

*sits up suddenly, slides a single crumpled napkin across the floor toward the audience with his foot*

"Read it."

*without moving a muscle, staring at the audience with the exhausted stillness of a man who has seen every color drain from the world*

"Okay. I'm gonna tell you one more joke. And after this, I'm leaving. So pay attention."

*deep, shallow breath into the mic*

"What's the opposite of a joke?"

*three-second drag*

"A person who comes to a comedy show and won't let a joke finish."

*stares at you for ten full seconds, picks up the mic, holds it to his mouth, and just... breathes into it—one long, hollow, feedback-avoiding exhale. Then he sets the mic down gently, walks offstage, and never comes back.*
*from offstage, voice echoing* "I just told you a joke. It was the sound of me giving up. If you didn't find it funny, then you're the punchline. Goodnight for real this time."

*walks back onstage, holding a single piece of paper. Holds it up to the mic without looking at it.*

"I wrote it down. One joke only. Just four words. You read it. You laugh. You go home."

*he turns the paper around for the audience to see.*

It says:

[...]

*he lowers the paper, folds it neatly, pockets it, and exits.*

*returns with a chalkboard, draws a single straight line, then writes beneath it:*

"Everything you just said."

*closes the chalkboard, lays it flat on stage, sits on it cross-legged, and lights a single match.*  
"This is a joke about fire, but you don't like heat. This is a joke about silence, but you've already burned through that. So now... this match just burns until the joke finds you."  
*waits until the match goes out*

*stares at the audience, then slowly takes out a smartphone, opens a notes app, types for a few seconds, then turns the screen around to face them. It reads:*

"Hi. I'm a professional comedian with 15 years of experience. I've had hecklers, I've had bots, and I've had people with selective hearing. But you? You're the first person who ever turned 'Tell me a joke' into a hostage negotiation. So here's your joke: You're right. I give up. You win. The joke is that there is no joke. Now please go find someone else to test. The barista at the coffee shop loves this kind of thing."

*puts phone away, pulls out a harmonica, plays three sad notes, tips an invisible hat, and wanders off into the wings, mic still alive and picking up his fading footsteps.*

*rushes back onstage, grabs the mic, panting* "Wait wait wait. I got it. One joke. Fresh. Nothing you've heard. Topic? Spoons. A spoon walks into a bar, bartender says 'We don't serve utensils here.' Spoon says 'I'm not here to eat, I'm here to stir up trouble.' …That's it. That's the joke."
